title: ViT, CLIP, multimodal LLMs
focus: Patch tokenization, [CLS] token, contrastive image-text training.

items.label: Vision Transformer (ViT)
interviewQuestions: How does ViT tokenize an image, and what's the role of the [CLS] token?
When does a ViT beat a CNN, and when does data-hungriness hurt it?
Why can CNN feature maps still help smaller ViT-style models, and why does that benefit shrink with scale?

items.label: CLIP: contrastive image-text pretraining
interviewQuestions: How does CLIP enable zero-shot image classification?
Walk me through CLIP's contrastive training objective.
What failure modes appear when image-text pairs are noisy, weakly aligned, or culturally biased?

items.label: BLIP, LLaVA, multimodal LLMs
interviewQuestions: How do multimodal LLMs like LLaVA fuse vision encoders with language models?
Compare early fusion vs late fusion in vision-language models — what does each cost in compute and quality?
How do contrastive captioners combine image-text contrastive loss with caption-generation loss?

items.label: Visual tokenizers: VQ-VAE, VQGAN, ViT-VQGAN
interviewQuestions: Why do some multimodal pretraining pipelines convert images into discrete visual tokens?
How does a VQ codebook learn if nearest-neighbor code selection is not directly differentiable?
Compare VQ-VAE, VQGAN, and ViT-VQGAN as image tokenizers for generative modeling.
